creator: dave@daveg.ca
text:
router bgp <my AS>
 neighbor <my other BGP router> remote-as <my AS>
 neighbor <my other BGP router> description shikra
 neighbor <my other BGP router> next-hop-self
 neighbor <my other BGP router> soft-reconfiguration inbound
 neighbor <my other BGP router> distribute-list bogon_list in
 neighbor <my other BGP router> distribute-list bogon_list out

access-list bogon_list deny 10.0.0.0/8
access-list bogon_list deny 172.16.0.0/12
access-list bogon_list deny 192.168.0.0/16
access-list bogon_list deny 127.0.0.0/8
access-list bogon_list deny 224.0.0.0/8
access-list bogon_list permit any

... and with this configuration, it would send 0 prefixes to the other peer.

I've omitted other neighbor statements, my network statements and even the ipv6 portions of things.  The two BGP routers are on the same publicly addressable lan ... and ... as I said, the older version works.
